title: Fragments on Nous: Mind Orders All Things
description: Anaxagoras on Mind (Nous) as the cosmic ordering principle that set the universe in motion.
introduction: Anaxagoras introduced the concept of Nous (Mind) as a cosmic principle — unmixed, infinite, and self-governing — that initiated the rotation of the cosmos and continues to order all things. Aristotle would later say that Anaxagoras 'stood out like a sober man among drunkards' for recognizing the need for an intelligent cause. His influence runs through Plato's Demiurge, Aristotle's Unmoved Mover, and the Stoic Logos.

translation.original_text: νοῦς δέ ἐστιν ἄπειρον καὶ αὐτοκρατὲς καὶ μέμεικται οὐδενὶ χρήματι, ἀλλὰ μόνος αὐτὸς ἐπ᾿ ἐωυτοῦ ἐστιν... καὶ πάντα γε ὅσα ψυχὴν ἔχει, καὶ τὰ μείζω καὶ τὰ ἐλάσσω, πάντων νοῦς κρατεῖ.
translation.text: Mind is infinite and self-ruling and is mixed with no thing, but is alone by itself... And all things that have soul, both the greater and the smaller — Mind rules them all.

translation.original_text: καὶ τὴν περιχώρησιν ταύτην, ἣν νῦν περιχωρεῖ τά τε ἄστρα καὶ ὁ ἥλιος καὶ ἡ σελήνη... νοῦς ἐκίνησε.
translation.text: And this rotation in which the stars and the sun and the moon now revolve... Mind set it all in motion.
